---
name: Missing guideline or topic
about: Request catalog coverage for a recurring chart, task, audience, data setting, or design situation.
title: "[Missing topic]: "
assignees: ""
---

## Issue Type

Missing guideline or missing topic area.

Use this template when friction from a concrete use case points to catalog coverage that could help other readers, agents, or maintainers.

## Summary

Describe the catalog gap in one short paragraph. Name the chart, task, audience, data setting, or design situation that needs coverage.

## Evidence From Use

- Task or chart context:
- Retrieval path:
- Searches or labels tried:
- What was missing:
- Why this appears to be a catalog content issue rather than a tooling issue:

## Related Guideline Records

Use `None found` when no guideline covered the case. If any records partially apply, list verified ids.

- `<guideline-id>`: `<title>`
  - Exact-read command:
  - Relevant section:
  - Why it partially applies or fails to cover the case:
  - Citation output:

## Suggested Catalog Change

- Add a new guideline about:
- Candidate title or phrasing:
- Suggested labels or label families:
- Candidate source references, if known:

## Public Disclosure Check

- [ ] No private user data.
- [ ] No local filesystem paths.
- [ ] No unpublished screenshots or session artifacts unless intentionally attached.
- [ ] Any referenced guideline ids were verified with exact reads.
- [ ] Source-backed records were checked with `chartcoach catalog cite` when references are discussed.

## Uncertainty

What should a catalog maintainer check before accepting or drafting the guideline?
